title: Optionally expose ComfyUI port 8188 on the Pod for direct testing/debug
description: Today the Pod only exposes 8889 (the Cubric wrapper REST layer); internal ComfyUI on 127.0.0.1:8188 is private. That blocks the useful debug path of loading a workflow directly in the Pod's ComfyUI web UI to isolate app/wrapper behavior from raw ComfyUI behavior (came up in MPI-191: could not run LTX_t2v.json manually on our own image to split image-vs-app). Add an OPT-IN way to expose 8188 (RunPod template port add, or a Pod env flag the wrapper/start.sh honors to bind ComfyUI on 0.0.0.0 + open the port) for debug Pods only — NOT default (security: ComfyUI has no auth). Low-risk, big testing win. Scope: mpi-ci start.sh/wrapper + RunPod template port list. USER-only live ops.
activeSessionTitle: MPI-191/192 wrapper verdict — offline audit + direct-8188 A/B wiring
